name: Civil Engineering
description: Test your Civil Engineering fundamentals across structural, environmental, and construction domains.

questions.question: What is a statically determinate structure?
options: Reactions can be found using equilibrium equations alone
questions.correct_answer: Reactions can be found using equilibrium equations alone
questions.explanation: In statically determinate structures, all reactions and internal forces can be found using equilibrium equations.

questions.question: What is the standard curing period for concrete?
questions.explanation: Concrete achieves its design strength (characteristic strength) after 28 days of curing.

questions.question: Which test determines the bearing capacity of soil?
questions.explanation: Plate load test directly measures the bearing capacity of soil at the foundation level.

questions.question: What instrument is used for measuring horizontal and vertical angles?
questions.explanation: A theodolite is a precision instrument for measuring horizontal and vertical angles in surveying.

questions.question: BOD stands for?
questions.explanation: BOD measures the oxygen needed by microorganisms to decompose organic matter in water.

questions.question: What is the purpose of camber on a road surface?
questions.explanation: Camber is the transverse slope given to road surface to drain rainwater quickly.

questions.question: What is the minimum grade of concrete for RCC work as per IS code?
questions.explanation: As per IS 456:2000, the minimum grade of concrete for RCC work is M20.

questions.question: Reynolds number is used to determine?
questions.explanation: Reynolds number (Re) determines the flow regime — Re < 2000 is laminar, Re > 4000 is turbulent.

questions.question: CPM in project management stands for?
questions.explanation: CPM identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project duration.

questions.question: What type of connection provides both fixity and rotational resistance?
questions.explanation: Rigid connections transfer both forces and moments, providing full fixity.

questions.question: What does the SPT (Standard Penetration Test) measure?
questions.explanation: SPT measures the N-value (blow count) indicating soil density and strength.

questions.question: What is a hydrograph?
options: Graph showing discharge vs time at a point
questions.correct_answer: Graph showing discharge vs time at a point
questions.explanation: A hydrograph plots the rate of flow (discharge) versus time at a specific point in a river.

questions.question: A beam of span 6m carries a UDL of 10 kN/m. Maximum bending moment at center?
questions.explanation: For UDL, M_max = wL²/8 = 10 × 36 / 8 = 45 kNm.

questions.question: A concrete mix ratio is 1:2:4. For 1 bag of cement (50kg), how much coarse aggregate is needed?
questions.explanation: Ratio 1:2:4 means for 1 part cement (50kg), coarse aggregate = 4 × 50 = 200 kg.

questions.question: If all high-rise buildings need deep foundations, and Building X is a high-rise, then?
questions.explanation: Direct logical deduction: All high-rises need deep foundations, X is high-rise, so X needs deep foundations.

questions.question: A water tank of 5m × 4m × 3m dimensions. What is the volume of water it can hold?
questions.explanation: Volume = 5 × 4 × 3 = 60 m³. 1 m³ = 1000 liters. So 60,000 liters.

questions.question: A project estimated at 100 days is 60% complete after 70 days. Will it finish on time?
questions.explanation: At current rate: 60% in 70 days → 100% needs ~117 days. 17 days delay expected.

questions.question: What is BIM in modern civil engineering?
questions.explanation: BIM creates digital representations of buildings for planning, design, construction, and management.

questions.question: A client asks why the project cost increased. Best response?
options: Provide detailed cost breakdown with justification for changes
questions.correct_answer: Provide detailed cost breakdown with justification for changes
questions.explanation: Transparency with data builds trust and demonstrates professional project management.

questions.question: What is the first priority on any construction site?
questions.explanation: Worker safety is always the top priority, governed by safety regulations and ethical responsibility.
